en.extract:
The rufous-winged fulvetta (Schoeniparus castaneceps) is a bird species of the family Pellorneidae. Its common name is misleading, because it is not a close relative of the "typical" fulvettas, which are now in the genus Fulvetta.
The black-crowned fulvetta (S. klossi) was until recently included here as a subspecies.
This 11 cm long bird has a dark-streaked chestnut crown, white supercilium, brown upperparts and pale underparts. The wings show a striking contrast between the bright rufous primaries and the black coverts.
This is a noisy species with a rich warbled ti-du-di-du-di-du-di-du song  and wheezy tsi-tsi-tsi-tsi call. 
It is common in evergreen montane forests above 1200 m ASL, often feeding on vertical trunks.


References

Collar, N.J. & Robson, Craig (2007): Family Timaliidae (Babblers). In: del Hoyo, Josep; Elliott, Andrew & Christie, D.A. (eds.): Handbook of Birds of the World, Volume 12 (Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees): 70-291. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.
Lekagul, Boonsong & Round, Philip (1991): A Guide to the Birds of Thailand. Saha Karn Baet. ISBN 974-85673-6-2
Robson, Craig (2004): A Field Guide to the Birds of Thailand. New Holland Press. ISBN 1-84330-921-1

The '''rufous-winged fulvetta''' ('''''Schoeniparus castaneceps''''') is a [[bird]] [[species]] of the [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Pellorneidae]].<ref name="iucn status 17 November 2021" /><ref name=ioc10.2>{{BioRef|IOC|version=version 10.2|family=Pellorneidae |access-date=25 July 2020}}</ref> Its common name is misleading, because it is not a close relative of the "typical" fulvettas, which are now in the [[genus]] ''[[Fulvetta]]''.

The [[black-crowned fulvetta]] (''S. klossi'') was until recently included here as a [[subspecies]].

This 11 cm long bird has a dark-streaked chestnut crown, white supercilium, brown upperparts and pale underparts. The wings show a striking contrast between the bright rufous primaries and the black [[covert (feather)|covert]]s.

This is a noisy species with a rich warbled ''ti-du-di-du-di-du-di-du'' [[Bird vocalization|song]]  and wheezy ''tsi-tsi-tsi-tsi'' call. 
<!--
It is found from [[India]]] west to [[China]], Malaysia]], [[Myanmar]], and [[Vietnam]].
[check following split]
-->

It is common in evergreen [[montane forest]]s above 1200 m [[Above mean sea level|ASL]], often feeding on vertical trunks.

[[File:Rufous-winged Fulvetta Khangchendzonga Biosphere Reserve West Sikkim India 17.02.2016.jpg|thumb|left|Rufous-winged fulvetta from [[Khangchendzonga National Park|Khangchendzonga Biosphere Reserve]], [[West Sikkim]]]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}
* Collar, N.J. & Robson, Craig (2007): Family Timaliidae (Babblers). ''In:'' del Hoyo, Josep; Elliott, Andrew & Christie, D.A. (eds.): ''[[Handbook of Birds of the World]], Volume 12'' (Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees): 70-291. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.
* Lekagul, Boonsong & Round, Philip (1991): ''A Guide to the Birds of Thailand''. Saha Karn Baet. <small>{{ISBN|974-85673-6-2}}</small>
* Robson, Craig (2004): ''A Field Guide to the Birds of Thailand''. New Holland Press. <small>{{ISBN|1-84330-921-1}}</small>

zh.extract:
栗头雀鹛（学名：Schoeniparus castaneceps），是雀眉科乌线雀鹛属的一种，分布于不丹、孟加拉国、老挝、中国大陆、越南、印度、泰国、缅甸、马来西亚和尼泊尔。全球活动范围约为2,360,000平方千米。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
栗头雀鹛的平均体重约为12.5克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的湿润低地林和亚热带或热带的湿润山地林。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。


亚种
栗头雀鹛指名亚种（学名：Schoeniparus castaneceps castaneceps）。分布于尼泊尔、锡金、不丹、印度、缅甸、泰国以及中国大陆的西藏、云南等地。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。
栗头雀鹛云南亚种（学名：Schoeniparus castaneceps exul）。分布于泰国、老挝、越南以及中国大陆的云南等地。该物种的模式产地在老挝PhuKong-Ntoul,BolovensPlateau。
